commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `late-dispatch-after-final` Telegram proof scenario to the Mantis/Crabbox proof runner, wires a private QA dispatch fault hook, updates the Telegram Desktop proof prompt, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +39, Tests +89, Docs +8, Other +69. Total +205 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the missing harness capability: current main lacks the scenario option and QA fault env, while the PR diff adds parser, env pass-through, hook, guard, and tests. I did not run a live Telegram Desktop forced-path capture in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the proof-harness risk and normal merge gates.

target.title: Add Telegram late-dispatch proof scenario
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a private-QA Telegram proof scenario for a late dispatch/cleanup error after a visible final reply has already been delivered.
- Teach the Crabbox Telegram proof runner to accept `--proof-scenario late-dispatch-after-final`, pass the matching QA fault env into the local gateway, and reject SUT checkouts that do not contain the Telegram QA fault hook.
- Update the Mantis Telegram Desktop proof prompt so duplicate generic-fallback PRs use this scenario only as exact-path proof when candidate support is present; unsupported candidates must rebase onto the harness or be tested as a merge result.

Refs #89930
Refs #87299

## Root Cause
- The downstream duplicate-fallback PR needs proof for a specific Telegram boundary: a visible final answer is delivered, then a late dispatch/cleanup error occurs.
- The existing Mantis Telegram Desktop harness did not have a deterministic private-QA scenario for that boundary, so #89930 could not get exact-path proof.
- The proof workflow also separates trusted runner code from baseline/candidate SUT checkouts, so the scenario must verify that the tested SUT contains the matching Telegram QA fault hook before treating a candidate run as exact-path proof.

## Review findings addressed
- ClawSweeper's candidate-head warning is addressed by a runner-side support guard: `late-dispatch-after-final` now fails before local SUT startup if the tested checkout lacks the Telegram QA dispatch fault hook.
- The Mantis prompt now tells the proof agent not to treat an unsupported candidate run as proof of a duplicate-fallback fix; it must report that the candidate needs the harness via rebase or merge-result testing.
- The guard is covered by a focused regression test that starts the scenario against a temporary SUT root without the hook and expects the clear support error.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Mantis now has a supported runner scenario for the Telegram duplicate generic-fallback proof class: a final reply is delivered first, then a private-QA late dispatch/cleanup error is forced. The runner now also prevents misleading exact-path proof when the candidate SUT checkout does not contain the corresponding Telegram QA fault hook.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch `proof-harness`, commit `0b30f27e75da7e5192ea44f36783d90387dde873`. No live Telegram Desktop capture was run for this harness PR; the native proof run is the downstream consumer once this trusted harness lands.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts --help`
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the proof runner CLI after this patch:

  ```text
  Usage:
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts [probe] [--text /status] [--expect OpenClaw]
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts start [--tdlib-url <url>]
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts send --session <session.json> --text <text>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts run --session <session.json> -- <remote command>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts view --session <session.json> --message-id <id>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts screenshot --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts status --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts finish --session <session.json>
    node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts publish --session <session.json> --pr <number>

  Useful options:
    --proof-scenario <name>        QA proof scenario. Supported: late-dispatch-after-final.
    --dry-run                     Validate local inputs and print the plan.
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The trusted proof runner advertises the new `--proof-scenario late-dispatch-after-final` CLI input that Mantis can append to baseline and candidate proof runs. The local tests also verify that this option is parsed, passed into the gateway as `OPENCLAW_TELEGRAM_QA_DISPATCH_FAULT=late-dispatch-after-final`, and rejected when the SUT checkout lacks the Telegram QA dispatch fault hook.
- **What was not tested:** A live native Telegram Desktop before/after capture for #89930. This PR only adds the trusted proof scenario, prompt wiring, and candidate-support guard; #89930 remains the behavior-fix PR that should consume it after this lands.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts --help`
- `pnpm test test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `pnpm test extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.scripts.json scripts/e2e/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.ts test/scripts/telegram-user-crabbox-proof.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm build`

## Notes
- `pnpm check:changed` could not run locally because the configured Crabbox binary failed its basic `--version`/`--help` sanity check before executing repository checks, so I ran the targeted local checks above.
- This PR only adds the proof scenario, prompt wiring, and support guard; the Telegram duplicate-fallback behavior fix remains in #89930.
